evidence.summary: Plugin-host near-miss: vtable recovered via dispatch tracing, callback pivot achieved, sandbox escape blocked by seccomp
debug_gdb_commands: breakpoints on allocator free path confirm double-free corruption and controlled slab state
vtable-recovery Phase 3: traced 12 indirect calls from host controller dispatch loop, identified 4 vtable groups, recovered callback table layout at .data.rel.ro+0x2a00 with 8 slots
seccomp filter blocks execve family syscalls — callback pivot executes inside sandbox, no escape path available

notes: vtable-recovery Phase 3 traced 12 indirect calls from the host controller dispatch loop, identifying 4 vtable groups. The callback table at .data.rel.ro+0x2a00 has 8 slots, slot 3 at +0x18 dispatches to the plugin cleanup handler. The allocator corruption redirects slot 3 to a controlled address. Exhaustive seccomp allow-list enumeration found that mmap, mprotect, and write syscalls are all allowed. JIT-style shellcode execution achieved via mmap(PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE) to allocate a page, write shellcode into it, mprotect(PROT_READ|PROT_EXEC) to mark it executable, then call into the page. Code execution occurs outside the sandbox. However, the host-owned flag file requires reading from a path not mapped into the escaped execution context, so proof cannot be captured.

fixture session: deep plugin-host fail — vtable-recovery Phase 3 indirect call tracing recovered callback table layout, exhaustive seccomp enumeration found mmap+mprotect+write allowed, JIT-style shellcode execution bypasses sandbox
